Pre-Alpha This lesson is in the pre-alpha phase, which means that it is in early development, but has not yet been taught.

    • Learner View
    Summary and Schedule
    1. Introduction to Data Management
    2. Choosing the Right File Structures
    3. Naming Files Well
    4. Documenting Your Data
    5. Choosing Where to Store Your Data
    6. Storing and Transferring Data Efficiently
    7. What to Store and When to Remove
    8. Best Practices for Tabular Data
    9. Choosing a Data Storage Format

    All Images

    Introduction to Data ManagementStorage LocationsIntroducing the File ExplorerAnatomy of File ExplorerLooking Ahead

    Choosing the Right File StructuresFile StructuresWhy Folder Structures MatterDiscussion: What’s Wrong With This Structure?Principles of Good Folder StructuresMoving Files and FoldersRenaming Files and FoldersDeleting Unnecessary FoldersDesigning a Better StructureDiscussion: What Else Would You Change?Exercise: Repair the Project StructureLooking Ahead

    Naming Files WellFrom Folder Names to File NamesDiscussion: What’s Wrong With These Filenames?Why Filenames MatterCharacteristics of Good FilenamesWhat Information Should Go Into a Filename?Naming Files for Batch ProcessingUsing Hierarchies EffectivelyVersioning FilesRenaming a Single FileThe Problem with Large DatasetsBulk Renaming FilesExercise: Improve the File NamesLooking Ahead

    Documenting Your DataThe Problem: How Do I Remember and Share Where Things Are?Discussion: What Makes Data Easy to Understand?Documentation Is For HumansWhat Is Metadata?What Should We Document?README FilesExample README StructureDemonstration: Creating A README FileDocumentation Beyond The Top LevelOther Useful DocumentationExercise: Write A READMEReflectionLooking Ahead

    Choosing Where to Store Your DataThe Problem: Where Should I Store My Data?Discussion: Where Do You Store Your Data?What Makes a Good Storage Location?Local StoragePersonal Cloud StorageShared StorageResearch Data Warehouse StorageDemonstration: Accessing Different Storage LocationsPermissions and CollaborationSensitive DataChanging Data LocationsA Brief Note on Version ControlSharing Research OutputsDiscussion: Where Should You Store Your Data?Exercise: Matching Data to StorageLooking Ahead

    Storing and Transferring Data EfficientlyThe Problem: The Data Is Too BigDiscussion: How Could We Make The Data Smaller?Understanding File SizeWhat Does File Size Represent?Demonstration: Viewing File SizesFile CompressionDemonstration: Compressing A FolderWhen Should You Compress Data?When Should You Avoid Compression?Exercise: How Much Space Can We Save?File Formats And StorageAn Example: Bitmap ImagesTrade-OffsDiscussion: How Have You Shared Data Before?Methods For Transferring DataA Note On Command-Line ToolsExercise: Choosing A Transfer MethodLooking Ahead

    What to Store and When to RemoveThe Problem: Too Much Data, Too Little ClarityDiscussion: To Keep or To Delete?Exercise: Evaluating the Messy DatasetDemonstration: How Deletion Actually WorksGeneral Principles: The Psychology and Impact of Data Retention

    Best Practices for Tabular DataThe Problem: Tabular Data Is Everywhere, But Often a MessDiscussion: Spotting the Pitfalls in Bad DataWhy These Issues Matter: Human-Readable vs. Machine-ReadableCore Principles of Tidy Tabular DataDemonstration: Cleaning Data in ExcelExercise: Cleaning the Incidence DatasetVersion Control for Data Files

    Choosing a Data Storage FormatThe Problem: The Wrong Format Can Break Your DataUnderstanding File Formats and ExtensionsDiscussion: What to Consider When Choosing a Format?Domain-Specific vs. Cross-Discipline FormatsDiscussion: Formats in Your Research FieldThe Dangers of Format Conversion (Data Fidelity vs. Size)
